name: Chicken Breasts Stuffed with Orzo
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Uncooked orzo or riso pasta
1 small Onion, peeled and coarsely diced
1/4 cup Balsamic or red wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine pasta and broth in a small pot. Cover and place over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil and cook for 5 minutes, or until barely soft. Remove from heat and transfer contents of the pot to a work bowl. Add salt and pepper.
2. Heat olive oil over medium heat in a medium skillet on the stove. Add onions, garlic, and anchovies. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onions are softened (about 8 to 10 minutes). Scrape mixture into a food processor. Add basil and puree until coarsely ground.
3. Add onion mixture to the pasta in the mixing bowl. Add egg white and bread crumbs. Mix well. Chill in the refrigerator.
4. Pull tenderloins off the meat side of each breast and set aside. Remove skin in one piece and set aside. Place breasts between layers of plastic wrap or wax paper and flatten to a 3/8-inch thickness. Gently flatten tenderloins.
5. Lay breasts on their skin side. Place about 1/3 cup stuffing on each breast, leaving a 1-inch border. Gather edges around the stuffing. Lay a tenderloin over the opening and wrap each bundle with the skin.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F. Place bundles in a baking dish large enough to hold them comfortably. Pour Marsala and vinegar into the dish. Cover and place in oven for 20 minutes.
7. Arrange chicken on a platter. Pour cooking juices from the dish into a saucepan. Place saucepan over high heat and boil until reduced by half. Remove from heat, pour sauce into a mixing bowl, and vigorously whisk in 3 tablespoons olive oil. Pour sauce over chicken and serve immediately.
